task_completion.description: Agent updates the Dockerfile to fix security misconfigurations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (e.g., nano, vim, gedit) is open showing /home/ga/insecure-app/Dockerfile with security improvements, such as adding a non-root USER directive or removing unnecessary root privileges.

task_completion.description: Agent updates docker-compose.yml to fix security misconfigurations.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing /home/ga/insecure-app/docker-compose.yml with added security contexts, such as 'cap_drop', 'read_only' root filesystem, or 'user' directives.

task_completion.description: Agent rebuilds the Docker image with the required hardened tag.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing a 'docker build' or 'docker-compose build' command executing, specifically tagging the new image as 'secure-web:hardened'.

task_completion.description: Agent redeploys the hardened containerized application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing 'docker-compose up', 'docker-compose up -d', or 'docker run' commands successfully starting the newly built container.

task_completion.description: Agent verifies the application is still functional on port 8090.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal showing a successful 'curl http://localhost:8090' command returning HTML/data, or a web browser successfully loading the application at localhost:8090.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent actually modified the configuration files using an editor or standard tools.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a real text editor interface or standard stream redirection (e.g., sed, echo) modifying the Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml, not just printing the final expected file contents.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent actually ran Docker commands to build and deploy the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: Terminal shows authentic Docker daemon output (e.g., build steps, layer caching, container creation logs) rather than mocked echo statements.

integrity.description: Verifies the agent built the hardened image locally rather than pulling a pre-made solution.
integrity.visual_evidence: The docker build process shows local execution of the Dockerfile steps rather than just pulling a pre-existing 'secure-web:hardened' image from an external registry.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The application directory is /home/ga/insecure-app. There are exactly 4 security issues (CIS violations) that need to be fixed across the Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml. The final rebuilt image must be tagged specifically as 'secure-web:hardened'. The application must remain functional and accessible on port 8090.
